papers.title_en: An Efficient Newton Algorithm for Nonnegative Matrix Factorization with the Kullback-Leibler Divergence
papers.authors: Damien Lesens, Jérémy E. Cohen, Bora Uçar
papers.evidence: 高效牛顿算法用于KL散度NMF，提供低秩表示，可直接用于高光谱去噪
papers.tldr: 非负矩阵分解（NMF）是重要的无监督学习工具，其Kullback-Leibler散度版本（KL-NMF）尤其适用于泊松分布计数数据。现有算法多依赖可分离上界的迭代最小化，但已近性能上限。本文提出基于二阶泰勒展开的牛顿型算法，并推广HALS步骤来优化非可分离代理函数。新算法可证明收敛，在多种数据集上超越现有方法，为KL-NMF提供了高效且理论扎实的求解方案。
papers.abstract_en: Nonnegative Matrix Factorization (NMF) is a fundamental tool in unsupervised learning, which approximates a nonnegative matrix by the product of two low-rank nonnegative factors. The Kullback-Leibler (KL) divergence is best suited to measure the data to model discrepancy when the decomposed data sample follows a Poisson distribution, which is the case for count datasets such as term-document matrices or images. Most KL-NMF algorithms in the literature minimize a separable majorant of the loss to find their next iterate. We argue that this method has reached its limits and propose to use instead the second-order Taylor expansion of the loss, leading to a Newton-type method. We minimize this non-separable surrogate by proposing a generalization of the well-known HALS algorithm. This yields an efficient KL-NMF algorithm which provably converges and which competes favorably with state-of-the-art algorithms on a large variety of datasets.
